Title: Multiple glutathione-S-transferases detoxify diverse glucosinolate-based defenses of Brassicales plants in a generalist lepidopteran herbivore (Spodoptera littoralis)

Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GST gene families are candidate targets for pest control strategies aimed at weakening detoxification of Brassicales glucosinolate defenses. Because strong redundancy was observed, single-gene editing is unlikely to be sufficient; stacked disruption of GST subfamily members or RNAi/class-targeting approaches would be more practical. Deployment should consider host-plant glucosinolate profiles, especially aliphatic and benzenic glucosinolates that generate toxic ITCs.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Broadly expressed GSTs enable detoxification of diverse isothiocyanates (ITCs), supporting larval growth on glucosinolate-defended Brassicales plants; higher GST activity correlates with better performance on ITC-containing diets.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Whole-family recombinant enzyme screening of 34 cytosolic GSTs as His-tagged proteins; ITC-GSH conjugation assays; kinetic assays; tissue RT-qPCR; larval feeding assays on ITC diets and Arabidopsis lines with altered glucosinolate profiles; metabolomics of ITC conjugates.
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Larvae fed artificial diets containing 1 µmol/g ITCs (allyl, butyl, isobutyl/sec-butyl, 3MSOP, 4MSOB, benzyl, 2PE) or Arabidopsis thaliana lines differing in glucosinolate content (Col-0, myb28 myb29 KO, myb28 myb29 × cyp79b2 cyp79b3 KO, CYP79A2 KI).
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: GST-encoding genes are broadly expressed across larval midgut, integument, Malpighian tubules, and weakly in hemolymph; detoxification-relevant genes are especially abundant in midgut, Malpighian tubules, and integument.
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broad tissue expression across larval organs in Spodoptera littoralis.
Common_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Family members submitted to NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: Glutathione (substrate/cofactor); mercapturic acid pathway enzymes downstream not individually identified
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Non-targeted metabolomics (UHPLC-qTOF-MS); targeted LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling; no public omics accession for transcriptome provided; raw supporting data at Edmond DOI 10.17617/3.WAOR7H85
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: More than half of the 34 cytosolic GSTs actively conjugated ITCs, and most acted on multiple substrates. Delta, epsilon, and sigma classes were active; zeta, theta, and omega classes were largely inactive toward tested ITCs. Side-chain structure strongly affected both toxicity and GST activity.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Functional redundancy may mask phenotypes in single knockouts; constitutive GST deployment may entail metabolic cost, though the paper suggests this cost may be limited. Suppressing GST detoxification could increase susceptibility to multiple plant toxins, not just ITCs.
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Cytosolic glutathione S-transferase superfamily; classes delta, epsilon, sigma, zeta, theta, omega; two unclassified GSTs
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. A large fraction of the S. littoralis GST family catalyzes glutathione conjugation of Brassicales-derived ITCs. 2. GST activity profile across ITC side chains correlates with larval performance on toxic diets. 3. Broad constitutive expression across detoxification organs likely underpins the generalist herbivore’s ability to feed on glucosinolate-containing plants.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Isothiocyanate-glutathione conjugates and downstream mercapturic acid products (especially ITC-Cys); also ITC-lysine conjugates
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Midgut, hemolymph, integument, and especially frass
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduce electrophilic toxicity of plant isothiocyanates and facilitate excretion, thereby promoting herbivore survival and growth on Brassicales hosts.

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Mercapturic acid pathway / xenobiotic detoxification of glucosinolate-derived isothiocyanates
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTE9 is a prime candidate for functional disruption to sensitize larvae to allyl and benzyl ITCs. Because of redundancy, it should be combined with additional active GSTs in multiplex editing or RNAi stacks. Useful for pest management in Brassicales with high aliphatic/benzenic glucosinolate content.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Conjugates glutathione (GSH) to isothiocyanates, especially allyl ITC and also benzyl ITC, forming ITC-GSH conjugates that enter the mercapturic acid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: High GSTE9 activity promotes detoxification of allyl and benzyl ITCs, supporting larval tolerance to these glucosinolate hydrolysis products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant His-tagged enzyme assay across multiple ITCs; kinetic assays showing high Vmax and catalytic efficiency; PCA of substrate specificity; docking simulation.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly initiates formation of detoxified ITC-GSH conjugates that are converted to excretable mercapturic acid pathway products.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Artificial diet containing allyl ITC or benzyl ITC at 1 µmol/g; in vitro assays with variable ITC side chains.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Tissue-specific transcript abundance not explicitly highlighted among the strongest examples; GST epsilon class genes overall were broadly expressed, weak in hemolymph.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Broad epsilon-class expression across larval tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within submitted accession set PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Upstream / first committed detoxification conjugation step
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics (UHPLC-qTOF-MS; LC-MS/MS); RT-qPCR for GST expression; NCBI sequence submission range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: GSTE9 showed the highest specific activity toward allyl ITC and very high activity toward benzyl ITC; docking predicted stronger binding of benzyl/2PE ITCs than allyl/butyl ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial GSH conjugation branch of ITC detoxification
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-gene disruption may be compensated by other GSTs. Enzyme also likely contributes to detoxification of other electrophiles.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glutathione plus allyl ITC or benzyl ITC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control point for tested allyl ITC detoxification; not established as sole rate-limiting step in vivo
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTE9 had the highest specific activity toward allyl ITC among tested GSTs. 2. Kinetic analysis showed very high catalytic efficiency for allyl ITC and substantial activity toward benzyl ITC. 3. GSTE9 likely contributes strongly to broad ITC detoxification in S. littoralis.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ITC-GSH conjugates leading to ITC-Cys and related mercapturic acid products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Larval frass; also body tissues after feeding
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Detoxification conjugates of glucosinolate hydrolysis products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Decrease ITC toxicity and support herbivore feeding on Brassicales plants.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTE14 is a valuable secondary target to pair with GSTE9 for reducing detoxification capacity to allyl and benzyl ITCs. Multiplex perturbation is preferable to overcome redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GSH conjugation to allyl ITC and benzyl ITC, favoring allyl ITC kinetically.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances detoxification of allyl and benzyl ITCs; likely contributes to tolerance in tissues including Malpighian tubules.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays; kinetic measurements; PCA of specificity; tissue RT-qPCR.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Initiates direct conversion of ITCs into glutathione conjugates for downstream processing and excretion.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: In vitro assays with allyl and benzyl ITC; larvae exposed to ITC-containing diets.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in Malpighian tubules according to discussion summary.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: High in Malpighian tubules; epsilon genes broad overall.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RT-qPCR; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PCA highlighted notable specificity toward both allyl ITC and benzyl ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely functionally redundant with other epsilon and sigma GSTs.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glutathione plus allyl ITC or benzyl ITC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTE14 conjugates both allyl and benzyl ITCs with GSH. 2. Catalytic efficiency is higher for allyl ITC than benzyl ITC. 3. It is one of the important epsilon GSTs participating in broad ITC detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ITC-GSH conjugates leading to mercapturic acid pathway products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification and excretion of plant-derived ITCs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTE17 is a supportive target for combined knockdown of epsilon-class GSTs to reduce allyl ITC tolerance. Best used in stacked interventions rather than alone.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GSH conjugation to allyl ITC and benzyl ITC, with greater catalytic efficiency toward allyl ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to detoxification of allyl and benzyl ITCs and supports tolerance to Brassicales defenses.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assays; kinetic analysis; tissue expression discussion.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Direct formation of ITC-GSH conjugates for subsequent mercapturic acid pathway conversion.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: ITC-containing artificial diets and in vitro substrate assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in the midgut according to discussion summary.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RT-qPCR; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: One of several epsilon GSTs that may cooperate in vivo despite moderate individual kinetics.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Redundancy may blunt measurable phenotype after single-gene perturbation.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glutathione plus allyl ITC or benzyl ITC
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTE17 conjugates allyl and benzyl ITCs with GSH. 2. Catalytic efficiency is substantially higher for allyl ITC than benzyl ITC. 3. It likely acts with other epsilon GSTs in midgut detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: ITC-GSH conjugates and downstream ITC-Cys derivatives

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTD3 is a strong candidate for reducing benzyl ITC detoxification in pest control, particularly in plants accumulating benzenic glucosinolates. Should be stacked with sigma-class GST targets for robust effect.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GSH conjugation to allyl ITC and especially benzyl ITC, with markedly higher catalytic efficiency toward benzyl ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports detoxification of benzenic ITCs, correlating with better larval growth on benzyl ITC compared with 2PE ITC diets.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant GST assay; kinetic analysis; docking simulations; tissue expression data.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly generates benzyl ITC-GSH and related conjugates for downstream detoxification.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Artificial diet and in vitro assays with allyl and benzyl ITCs; benzyl ITC emphasized.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in integument than other organs; delta genes overall broadly expressed except weakly in hemolymph.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RT-qPCR; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Docking predicted stronger binding to benzyl/2PE ITCs than allyl/butyl ITCs; discussed as active toward volatile benzyl ITC in integument.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely also handles other xenobiotics; disrupting it may broaden sensitivity to multiple toxins.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glutathione plus benzyl ITC or allyl ITC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key contributor for benzyl ITC detoxification; not sole control step
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTD3 efficiently conjugates benzyl ITC with GSH. 2. Catalytic efficiency for benzyl ITC is much higher than for allyl ITC. 3. Its expression in integument suggests a role in protection from volatile benzenic ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Benzyl ITC-GSH and downstream detoxification conjugates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Neutralization and excretion of benzenic ITCs.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTS5 is a strong target to impair benzyl ITC detoxification and may be particularly relevant for exposure of outer tissues to volatile ITCs. Combined targeting with GSTD3 or GSTS6 is recommended.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GSH conjugation to allyl ITC and especially benzyl ITC; sigma-class enzyme showing high specificity toward benzenic ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes detoxification of benzyl ITC and likely helps protect larvae from volatile benzenic ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme assay; kinetic assay; PCA; docking simulation; tissue expression analysis.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Direct initial detoxification step converting benzyl ITC into GSH conjugate.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Benzyl ITC exposure in artificial diets and enzyme assays.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Most expressed in integument; sigma genes show strong tissue specificity.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RT-qPCR; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PCA showed high specificity toward benzyl ITC; docking revealed stronger hydrogen bonding with benzyl ITC than allyl ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: May participate in detoxification of additional electrophilic compounds beyond ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glutathione plus benzyl ITC or allyl ITC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch-preference determinant for benzenic ITC detoxification; not proven rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTS5 shows high activity and catalytic efficiency toward benzyl ITC. 2. It is one of the sigma GSTs preferentially detoxifying benzenic ITCs. 3. High integument expression suggests a defensive role against volatile external ITC exposure.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Benzyl ITC-GSH and downstream Cys/CysGly products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Frass; likely peripheral tissues after exposure
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxify benzenic ITCs and facilitate elimination.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTS6 is an especially attractive target because it combines strong benzyl ITC-detoxifying activity, midgut-enriched expression, and benzyl-ITC inducibility. Could be prioritized in RNAi or CRISPR stacks.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GSH conjugation to allyl ITC and benzyl ITC, with much higher catalytic efficiency toward benzyl ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports benzyl ITC detoxification and contributes to tolerance in the midgut; induced by benzyl ITC feeding.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant enzyme screening; kinetic analysis; RT-qPCR induction assay; tissue expression profiling; PCA.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms ITC-GSH conjugates entering the mercapturic acid pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Benzyl ITC feeding induces transcript accumulation in larval midgut; enzyme assays with allyl and benzyl ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in the midgut; induced about two-fold by benzyl ITC feeding in midgut.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RT-qPCR; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Among the few GST genes inducible by dietary ITCs; sigma class generally preferred benzenic ITCs over aliphatic ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely contributes to metabolism of other dietary electrophiles; knockout may have broader physiological effects.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Glutathione plus benzyl ITC or allyl ITC
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Important inducible contributor for benzyl ITC detoxification
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTS6 is highly expressed in the midgut and is induced by benzyl ITC feeding. 2. It shows substantially higher catalytic efficiency for benzyl ITC than allyl ITC. 3. GSTS6 likely plays a key physiological role in benzenic ITC detoxification in digestive tissues.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Benzyl ITC-GSH and downstream mercapturic acid conjugates
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Protects larval gut from benzenic ITC toxicity.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTE1 is notable for its rare activity toward methylsulfinyl ITCs such as 3MSOP ITC and 4MSOB ITC, the more growth-inhibitory Brassicales toxins. This makes it a strategic target for enhancing plant defense efficacy against generalist herbivores.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GSH conjugation to multiple ITCs and is one of the few GSTs with measurable activity toward methylsulfinyl-containing ITCs including 3MSOP ITC and 4MSOB ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes to detoxification of particularly toxic methylsulfinyl aliphatic ITCs, potentially buffering larvae against 4MSOB ITC-rich plants.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant screening assay across structurally diverse ITCs; tissue expression discussion.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Direct initial conjugation of highly toxic 4MSOB-type ITCs to GSH.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Exposure to 3MSOP and 4MSOB ITCs in enzyme assays; 4MSOB ITC-rich Arabidopsis and diet treatments relevant in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Mainly expressed in the midgut according to discussion summary.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RT-qPCR; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Most GSTs had little activity toward methylsulfinyl ITCs; GSTE1 was one of the few exceptions highlighted by the authors.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial GSH conjugation branch for methylsulfinyl ITCs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Single-gene targeting may only partially reduce 4MSOB ITC detoxification because other GSTs such as GSTE10 also show activity.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential bottleneck for methylsulfinyl ITC detoxification, but not definitively established
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTE1 is among the rare GSTs active toward methylsulfinyl ITCs. 2. This activity is relevant because 4MSOB ITC was the most growth-inhibitory ITC tested. 3. Midgut expression suggests a role in first-pass detoxification of aliphatic Brassicales toxins.

Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTE10 is another candidate to disrupt detoxification of highly toxic 4MSOB-type compounds; best deployed together with GSTE1 and other active GSTs.
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes GSH conjugation to multiple ITCs and, unlike most GSTs, retains activity toward methylsulfinyl-containing 3MSOP ITC and 4MSOB ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: May help larvae tolerate the most inhibitory tested ITC class, especially 4MSOB ITC.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Recombinant ITC screening assay; tissue expression discussion.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly generates GSH conjugates from methylsulfinyl ITCs.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: 4MSOB/3MSOP ITC exposure in vitro; relevance to aliphatic glucosinolate-rich plants in vivo.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Higher expression in integument compared with other organs according to discussion summary.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Metabolomics; RT-qPCR; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned as one of the few enzymes with activity on methylsulfinyl ITCs despite overall low family activity on these substrates.
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Initial GSH conjugation branch for methylsulfinyl ITCs
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Probably one of several overlapping GSTs; phenotype may be modest alone.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTE10 is one of the few S. littoralis GSTs active against methylsulfinyl ITCs. 2. Such substrates are especially toxic to larvae. 3. The enzyme may contribute to peripheral detoxification due to higher integument expression.
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Detoxification of particularly inhibitory aliphatic ITCs.

Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Native promoter; no transgenic promoter used in insect
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: As one of the few benzyl-ITC-inducible GST genes, GSTE6 is a good marker and candidate node for disrupting adaptive inducible detoxification. Alone it may not be sufficient; better as part of class-level suppression of epsilon GSTs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Transcript abundance increases about three-fold in larval midgut after benzyl ITC feeding, suggesting an inducible contribution to detoxification of benzenic ITCs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR of larval midgut after feeding on benzyl ITC, allyl ITC, 4MSOB ITC, or control diet.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller: one of only three GST genes induced by dietary ITCs and the strongest induced gene, indicating selective transcriptional responsiveness to benzyl ITC exposure.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Benzyl ITC feeding for 2 days induced expression; allyl ITC and 4MSOB ITC did not induce it.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Detected in larval tissues; inducible in midgut by benzyl ITC.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Detoxification/biosynthesis of GSH conjugates from benzyl ITC exposure
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; metabolomics context for ITC detoxification; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although induced, the paper does not provide kinetic characterization of GSTE6 protein toward benzyl ITC in detail.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Inducible expression may overlap with constitutively active GSTs, limiting standalone effect.
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: Not applicable; GSTE6 is itself a regulated detoxification enzyme rather than an upstream TF
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional induction by dietary benzyl ITC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTE6 transcript is specifically induced by benzyl ITC feeding. 2. It is among the very few GST genes showing dietary ITC responsiveness. 3. This indicates selective transcriptional adaptation to benzenic ITCs.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Reduce benzyl ITC toxicity during feeding.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTS3 can serve as an inducible sigma-class target for suppressing benzyl ITC-responsive detoxification. It is best combined with constitutively expressed sigma/delta GSTs for stronger phenotypic impact.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Midgut transcript level increases about two-fold upon benzyl ITC feeding, consistent with a role in benzyl ITC detoxification.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR induction analysis in larval midgut after ITC feeding.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Condition-dependent controller of benzyl ITC response; one of only three inducible GST genes among 34 tested.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Benzyl ITC feeding induced expression; allyl ITC and 4MSOB ITC did not.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Inducible in midgut by benzyl ITC; tissue-specific sigma expression overall.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Detoxification of benzenic ITCs via GSH conjugation pathway
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; metabolomics context; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The discussion notes GSTS3 has higher activity for benzyl ITC conjugation than for allyl ITC or 4MSOB ITC.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Likely redundant with other sigma GSTs, especially GSTS6 and GSTS5.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional induction by dietary benzyl ITC
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTS3 is specifically induced by benzyl ITC in larval midgut. 2. It likely contributes to benzenic ITC conjugation. 3. The induction pattern supports selective transcriptional tuning to side-chain chemistry.

Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: GSTS6 is a particularly high-value target because it is both constitutively enriched in the midgut and further induced by benzyl ITC. Silencing it could reduce first-pass detoxification of benzenic ITCs.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Midgut transcript increases about two-fold in response to benzyl ITC, reinforcing its physiological role in benzyl ITC detoxification.
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: RT-qPCR induction analysis; tissue expression profiling; enzyme kinetics showing high benzyl ITC activity.
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Strong condition-dependent controller and likely major physiological effector because it combines benzyl-ITC inducibility, midgut enrichment, and high catalytic efficiency for benzyl ITC.
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Benzyl ITC feeding induces expression; allyl ITC and 4MSOB ITC do not.
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Predominantly expressed in larval midgut and induced by benzyl ITC.
Regulation_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Not individually mapped in provided text; within PQ722158-PQ722184
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: Detoxification of benzenic ITCs via glutathione conjugation
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RT-qPCR; enzyme assay/kinetics; metabolomics context; NCBI accession range PQ722158-PQ722184
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Also listed under Pathway_Genes because the paper provides direct catalytic evidence; here included for its inducible regulatory behavior.
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Targeting may affect broader xenobiotic handling due to sigma GST multifunctionality.
Regulation_Genes.Regulation_Mode: Transcriptional induction by dietary benzyl ITC
Regulation_Genes.Regulator_Type: Inducible detoxification enzyme gene with direct catalytic role
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GSTS6 is one of only three GST genes induced by ITC feeding. 2. The induction is specific to benzyl ITC and occurs in the midgut. 3. Its high catalytic efficiency toward benzyl ITC makes it a likely physiologically important inducible detoxifier.
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Benzyl ITC-GSH and downstream mercapturic acid products
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Mitigate benzyl ITC toxicity and aid excretion.
